title: Form Item
description: The form item type: a container-like type that groups variable-widget children and packs them compactly so each widget occupies a label+control cell rather than consuming a whole main-grid placement. Like container, form is structurally special — it may carry `children` — but it normalizes to a FLOW layout (label+control rows, optionally split into N columns) rather than the weighted grid. The resolver (E2-S1) computes each child's flow cell from its document order and the form's column count; form children never declare or consume a main-grid placement. A form rejects non-widget children fail-fast (LAYOUT_FORM_CHILD_INVALID), and an out-of-range column count fails LAYOUT_FORM_COLUMNS_INVALID. The `layout.mode` discriminator currently supports only `flow`; the weighted-grid form mode is a separate story (E2-S2). NO CSS keywords or absolute units appear anywhere — columns is a unitless count.

layout.title: Form Layout
layout.description: The form's layout declaration. `mode` selects the arrangement (currently only `flow`, the compact label+control stacking; a future weighted-grid mode is added in E2-S2). Omit the whole object for a single-column flow.

mode.title: Layout Mode
mode.description: Discriminator selecting the form's arrangement. `flow` (the default and only current value) stacks each child into a label+control row, filling left-to-right across `columns` then wrapping. Structured so additional modes can be added without changing existing forms.

columns.title: Flow Columns
columns.description: Number of columns the flow arranges children into, as a unitless count. Children fill row-major (left-to-right, then wrap). Defaults to 1 (a single stacked column). NO CSS units or keywords.
